title: Slandering Chaste Women (Qadhf)
subtitle: Cursed in This World and the Hereafter for Accusing the Innocent

verses.arabic: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَرْمُونَ الْمُحْصَنَاتِ الْغَافِلَاتِ الْمُؤْمِنَاتِ لُعِنُوا فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ
verses.translation: Indeed, those who [falsely] accuse chaste, unaware and believing women are cursed in this world and the Hereafter; and they will have a great punishment.

verses.arabic: وَالَّذِينَ يَرْمُونَ الْمُحْصَنَاتِ ثُمَّ لَمْ يَأْتُوا بِأَرْبَعَةِ شُهَدَاءَ فَاجْلِدُوهُمْ ثَمَانِينَ جَلْدَةً وَلَا تَقْبَلُوا لَهُمْ شَهَادَةً أَبَدًا ۚ وَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْفَاسِقُونَ
verses.translation: And those who accuse chaste women and then do not produce four witnesses — lash them with eighty lashes and do not accept from them testimony ever after. And those are the defiantly disobedient.

verses.arabic: إِلَّا الَّذِينَ تَابُوا مِن بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ وَأَصْلَحُوا فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
verses.translation: Except for those who repent thereafter and reform themselves, for indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.

brief_explanation: Qadhf — falsely accusing a chaste believing woman (or man) of fornication — is one of the few sins in the Quran explicitly described as earning the curse of Allah in both this world and the Hereafter, alongside a 'great punishment.' The Quran prescribes 80 lashes and permanent disqualification of testimony as worldly consequences, and brands the slanderer a 'fasiq' (immoral transgressor) for life unless they repent. This sin destroys reputations, tears families apart, and spreads fawahish (indecencies) through society — the very opposite of the believing community's responsibility to guard one another's honour. Allah revealed an entire surah (An-Nur) in response to the slander against Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her), establishing this protection for all believers.
capital_punishment: The prescribed hadd for qadhf is 80 lashes, not execution. This is agreed upon by all four madhabs (Hanafi, Maliki, Shafi'i, Hanbali) and is established directly in the Quran (24:4). In addition to the lashing, the slanderer's testimony is permanently rejected unless they repent, and they are publicly declared a fasiq. The punishment is applied upon the demand of the victim; if the victim pardons the slanderer before the punishment is carried out, the hadd is dropped according to the majority of scholars.
